decisions.title: Expanded RPC usage accounting and thinking introspection
decisions.rationale: Pi now includes tool and summary usage in get_session_stats and exposes get_available_thinking_levels (upstream docs/rpc.md); ce-workflow already consumes before/after session stats and verifies the applied thinking level in scripts/workflow-evaluation-rpc.mjs, so the installed runtime provides the benefit without another compatibility layer.

decisions.title: Tool and compaction usage metadata
decisions.rationale: The new optional usage fields are additive (upstream docs/extensions.md and docs/compaction.md). ce-workflow's instant compaction performs no nested model call, while subagent costs remain deliberately separated in work telemetry, so no usage-producing call site needs a change.

decisions.title: Native provider registration, Qwen plans, and llama.cpp management
decisions.rationale: ce-workflow registers workflow tools and commands but no model provider; repository searches found no registerProvider or ModelRuntime integration point, so provider-owned authentication and local model management do not simplify this package.

decisions.title: Additive extension lifecycle and public type exports
decisions.rationale: 0.81.0 adds root type exports and hidden inline-extension metadata without removing the lifecycle hooks ce-workflow uses; extensions/work-models.js remains on supported agent_end, agent_settled, turn_end, and tool lifecycle contracts.

decisions.title: Project-grounded proactive catch-up verdicts
decisions.rationale: ce-pov turns upstream release notes into repository-specific adoption decisions before the baseline advances.
decisions.verification: work-catch-up objective and completion-gate tests passed

decisions.title: Technical explainers for difficult adoption choices
decisions.rationale: ce-explain is useful only when a candidate cannot be decided from the compact POV summary.
decisions.verification: work-catch-up objective test covers conditional ce-explain guidance

decisions.title: Recurring external feedback sweep
decisions.rationale: ce-sweep overlaps the native work-item and improvement-reporting flow until Slack, GitHub Issues, or email ingestion is explicitly configured.

decisions.title: Current wait and acceptance contracts
decisions.rationale: Direct handoffs should use subagent_wait guidance and reason-bearing acceptance instead of deprecated shorthand.
decisions.verification: coded work start/finish behavior and npm run verify:quiet passed

decisions.title: Selective optional comments
decisions.rationale: Planning and adoption choices benefit from caveats, while destructive binary approvals must explicitly disable comments.
decisions.verification: open-question, work-goal, and dirty-recovery tests passed

decisions.title: Diagnostic disposition tool
decisions.rationale: pi-lens now registers lens_diagnostic_mark as a situational tool and honors its marks across diagnostics (upstream docs/dispositions.md). The host exposes it automatically through pi_lens_activate_tools, while ce-workflow's verifier triage covers a separate review-finding store, so duplicate integration would add no value.

decisions.title: Deferred formatter and autofix bus signals
decisions.rationale: The new pilens:format:queued, pilens:format:start, and pilens:autofix:start events are useful only for an in-process immutable-snapshot listener. ce-workflow schedules background verification from an explicit settled checkpoint in extensions/work-models.js rather than snapshotting during deferred lens mutation, so there is no current race to fix.

decisions.title: Cached workspace diagnostics and scan correctness fixes
decisions.rationale: Persisted LSP sweep caching, tracked-file discovery, source-twin resolution, deduplication, and warm-up timeout fixes are internal pi-lens behavior (upstream CHANGELOG.md); ce-workflow already calls the public lens diagnostics tools and inherits the faster, quieter results automatically.

decisions.title: Optional ty server and human project map
decisions.rationale: ty is a PATH-only fallback selected inside pi-lens and /lens-map is a human-only command with no agent tool. Neither has a ce-workflow call site or requires repository configuration.
